key_insight: Machine-learning-driven hybrid Monte Carlo/molecular dynamics shows that chemical ordering in (MoCrTi)$_{100-x}$Al$_x$ refractory high-entropy alloys can produce one or two order-disorder transitions and strongly alter stiffness.
engineering_relevance: By tuning Al content to control B2-type ordering, designers can target higher elastic moduli and non-monotonic stiffness peaks for high-temperature refractory alloy applications.
method: Hybrid Monte Carlo + molecular dynamics simulations
Analyze heat capacity, short-range order, and elastic constants across compositions and temperatures
evidence_signal: The abstract reports temperature-dependent heat-capacity and short-range-order signatures, plus composition-dependent elastic constants/moduli, to distinguish single versus two-stage ordering transitions.
watch_item: The conclusions depend on the accuracy and transferability of the machine-learning potential and the extent to which the simulated compositions and temperatures represent real processing conditions.
paper_title: Study of ordering in (MoCrTi)$_{100-x}$Al$_x$ refractory high-entropy alloys using machine learning interatomic potential
